title: Error when podman run with --uidmap
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

**kind bug**

**Description**

Playing and having fun with podman for self host some service, uidmap does't seem to work as expected.

As root user, executing the following command (from[https://www.projectatomic.io/blog/2018/05/podman-userns/](https://www.projectatomic.io/blog/2018/05/podman-userns/)) :

`podman run --uidmap=0:100000:70000 --gidmap=0:200000:70000 fedora-minimal cat /proc/self/uid_map`

Give me an error:

```
container create failed: container_linux.go:344: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:424: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:46: preparing rootfs caused \\\"permission denied\\\"\""
: internal libpod error
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**



**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.12.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.11.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.6-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.12.2-20.dev.git7438b7b.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 0baa327e6bb6ec49589ca8b82de24058ae171702'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "29"
  MemFree: 7019593728
  MemTotal: 33654996992
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-66.dev.gitbbb17ef.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: ead425507b6ba28278ef71ad06582df97f2d5b5f
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 16922288128
  SwapTotal: 16924012544
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 16
  hostname: theden
  kernel: 4.19.10-300.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 58h 39m 15.81s (Approximately 2.42 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 29 with podman install from rawhide

comments.body: Are you running Podman as root, or rootless?

> Are you running Podman as root, or rootless?

As root

Works for me fine on F29.

Could you check if you have an issue with SELinux.

Have you tried this command on a fresh storage or was it created by some older podman versions?  It could be an error in the chowned image, in that case a `rm -rf /var/lib/containers/storage` should help
also, could you check if `/run/libpod/containers-root` has mode 0755?

> also, could you check if `/run/libpod/containers-root` has mode 0755?

Nope /run/libpod/containers-root and subdirectories are created with 0700

`
rm -Rf /var/lib/containers

rm -Rf /run/libpod/containers

podman run --uidmap=0:100000:70000 --gidmap=0:200000:70000 fedora-minimal cat /proc/self/uid_map

ls -dlZ /run/libpod/containers-root/

drwx------. 3 root root unconfined_u:object_r:container_var_run_t:s0 60  4 janv. 13:26 /run/libpod/containers-root/`
After cleanup did the podman run still fail?

We expect root filesystems to land in /var/lib/containers/storage - I don't actually know what /run/libpod/containers-root would be doing, unless you explicitly specified that as the containers/storage root
Did you modify any paths in /etc/containers/storage.conf or /etc/containers/libpod.conf?

>
>
>After cleanup did the podman run still fail?

Still fail after clean up /var/lib/containers and /run/libpod/containers

On an other fresh VM install (Fedora 29), command (`podman run --uidmap=0:100000:70000 --gidmap=0:200000:70000 fedora-minimal cat /proc/self/uid_map`) works and /run/libpod/containers is created with 0755 permission. I can't figure out why it is different on my main system.

>
>
> Did you modify any paths in /etc/containers/storage.conf or /etc/containers/libpod.conf?

I don't remember changing anything in the configuration file...
We are using that to bind mount the rootfs from the storage as uid!=0 has no permissions to use it. The error, I think, depends on it being 0700 instead of 0755

My root umask was set to 077, this is why /run/libpod/containers-root was created with 700. 
Changing the umask to 022 make /run/libpod/containers-root be created with 755, in that case I can run successfully `podman run --uidmap=0:100000:70000 --gidmap=0:200000:70000 fedora-minimal cat /proc/self/uid_map`.
Seems like something we need to handle within podman then.
